evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: accuracy, compliance, scalability, and long-term usability
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour signals dominate — accuracy, compliance, scalability, high availability, monitoring — with zero velocity phrases; classified as rigour.
evidence.domain_risk: gaming KPIs and user profiles; compliance
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Gaming/entertainment sector; 'compliance' appears in context of data usability not regulatory obligations; errors affect business decisions not financial reporting; classified as moderate.
evidence.data_team_maturity: large data sets across multiple teams
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Experience with 'large data sets across multiple teams' and code review standards suggest an established and growing data function; DBT in requirements not responsibilities limits the signal; classified as mid.
evidence.jd_authorship: extract, clean, and transform data from various sources; collaborate with data scientists to provide reliable datasets
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Responsibilities are generic boilerplate applicable to any analytics engineer role — no named tools in responsibilities, no scale numbers, Tuesday morning not legible; classified as recruiter.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Primary outputs are gaming KPIs and user profiles in service of product analytics; classified as product.
evidence.autonomy_level: collaborate with data scientists to provide reliable datasets
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role receives scoped work and delivers datasets and models in support of data scientists; no strategic direction-setting language; classified as execution.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: design data models capturing gaming KPIs; monitoring pipelines for high availability and performance
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Mix of designing new models (greenfield) and monitoring existing pipelines (fix_scale) with neither dominant; classified as mixed.
evidence.work_arrangement: hybrid model requires office presence Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The JD lists standard data engineering tools (dbt, GCP, Infrastructure as Code) with no mention of AI coding tools or GenAI-consuming infrastructure.
evidence.testing_framing: collaborate with data scientists to provide reliable datasets
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Reliability is described as a collaborative outcome rather than an explicitly owned testing or quality practice, matching the absent classification despite the accuracy/compliance language elsewhere.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: ensuring accuracy, compliance, scalability, and long-term usability; monitoring pipelines for high availability and performance
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Monitoring, high availability, and reliability language reflect operational risk concern secondary to the JD's overall delivery focus, consistent with moderate loss aversion.
